heading: Capital loss carrybacks and carryovers
label: 1212 Capital loss carrybacks and carryovers
notes: Amendments2010—Subsec. (a)(1)(C). , amended subpar. (C) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (C) read as follows: “a capital loss carryover—Pub. L. 111–325, § 101(b)(1)“(i) in the case of a regulated investment company (as defined in section 851) to each of the 8 taxable years succeeding the loss year, and“(ii) to the extent such loss is attributable to a foreign expropriation capital loss, to each of the 10 taxable years succeeding the loss year.”Subsec. (a)(3), (4). , added par. (3) and redesignated former par. (3) as (4).Pub. L. 111–325, § 101(a)2004—Subsec. (a)(3). reenacted heading without change and amended text of par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (3) provided that a net capital loss of a corporation would not be carried back under par. (1)(A) to a taxable year for which it was a foreign personal holding company (as defined in section 552), for which it was a regulated investment company (as defined in section 851), for which it was a real estate investment trust (as defined in section 856), or for which an election made by it under section 1247 was applicable (relating to election by foreign investment companies to distribute income currently).Pub. L. 108–3571988—Subsec. (b)(2). substituted “Treatment of amounts allowed under section 1211(b)(1) or (2)” for “Special rule” as heading and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of determining the excess referred to in subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (1), an amount equal to the amount allowed for the taxable year under paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1211(b) shall be treated as a short-term capital gain in such year.”Pub. L. 100–6471986—Subsec. (b)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2), special rules, read as follows:Pub. L. 99–514, § 301(b)(11)“(A) For purposes of determining the excess referred to in paragraph (1)(A), an amount equal to the amount allowed for the taxable year under section 1211(b)(1)(A), (B), or (C) shall be treated as a short-term capital gain in such year.“(B) For purposes of determining the excess referred to in paragraph (1)(B), an amount equal to the sum of—“(i) the amount allowed for the taxable year under section 1211(b)(1)(A), (B), or (C), and“(ii) the excess of the amount described in clause (i) over the net short-term capital loss (determined without regard to this subsection) for such year,shall be treated as a short-term capital gain in such year.”Subsec. (c)(6)(B), (7)(A). , amended directory language of , resulting in amendment of subsec. (c)(6)(B). See 1984 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1899A(67)Pub. L. 98–369, § 102(e)(3)(C)1984—Subsec. (b)(3). , struck out par. (3) which read as follows: “In the case of any amount which, under paragraph (1) and section 1211(b) (as in effect for taxable years beginning before ), is treated as a capital loss in the first taxable year beginning after , paragraph (1) and section 1211(b) (as in effect for taxable years beginning before ) shall apply (and paragraph (1) and section 1211(b) as in effect for taxable years beginning after , shall not apply) to the extent such amount exceeds the total of any net capital gains (determined without regard to this subsection) of taxable years beginning after .”Pub. L. 98–369, § 1002(a)January 1, 1970December 31, 1969January 1, 1970December 31, 1969December 31, 1969Subsec. (c). , (B), substituted “net section 1256 contracts loss” for “net commodity futures loss” and “section 1256 contracts” for “regulated futures contracts” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 98–369, § 102(e)(3)(A)Subsec. (c)(3)(A), (5). , substituted “net section 1256 contract gain” for “net commodity futures gain” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 98–369, § 102(e)(3)(D)Subsec. (c)(6)(B), (7)(A). , as amended by , substituted “section 1256 contract” for “regulated futures contract” wherever appearing.Pub. L. 98–369, § 102(e)(3)(C)Pub. L. 99–514, § 1899A(67)1983—Subsec. (c)(4)(A). struck out “and positions to which section 1256 applies” after “losses from regulated futures contracts”.Pub. L. 97–4481982—Subsec. (a)(3), (4). struck out par. (3) relating to electing small business corporations, and redesignated par. (4) as (3).Pub. L. 97–3541981—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 97–341978—Subsec. (a)(1)(C)(ii). substituted “succeeding the loss year” for “exceeding the loss year”.Pub. L. 95–6001976—Subsec. (a)(1). , §§ 1403(a), 1901(b)(33)(O), in subpar. (B) inserted introductory text “except as provided in subparagraph (C),” and struck out “(10) taxable years to the extent such loss is attributable to a foreign expropriation capital loss)” after “5 taxable years” and added subpar. (C), and substituted “capital gain net income” for “net capital gains”, “net capital gain” and “net capital gain” in last three sentences, respectively.Pub. L. 94–4551969—, substituted “carrybacks and carryovers” for “carryover” in section catchline.Pub. L. 91–172, § 512(f)(1)Subsec. (a)(1). , provided for a 3-year capital loss carryback for corporations, not available for foreign expropriation capital losses for which a special 10-year carryforward is presently available, in addition to the 5–year capital loss carryforward presently allowed corporations, to the extent the carryback of such loss does not increase or produce a net operating loss for the taxable year to which it is being carried back.Pub. L. 91–172, § 512(a)Subsec. (a)(3), (4). , added pars. (3) and (4).Pub. L. 91–172, § 512(b)Subsec. (b). , struck out reference to , struck out determination of a short-term capital gain as an amount equal to the excess allowed for the taxable year under former section 1211(b) over the gains from sales or exchanges of capital assets, struck out par. (2) treating as a short-term capital loss in the first taxable year beginning after , any amount which is treated as a short-term capital loss in such year under this subchapter as in effect immediately before the enactment of the Revenue Act of 1964, added new par. (2) dealing with special rules for determining the excesses referred to in par. (1)(A) and par. (1)(B) and added par. (3).Pub. L. 91–172, § 513(b)Dec. 31, 1963Dec. 31, 19631964—Subsec. (a). provided that if any portion of a net capital loss is attributable to a foreign expropriation capital loss, such portion shall be a short-term capital loss in each of the 10 succeeding taxable years, defined foreign expropriation capital loss, stated what portion of loss is attributable to foreign expropriation capital loss and the priority of application of the net capital loss, and struck out provisions that net capital losses for taxable years beginning before , were to be determined under the applicable law relating to the computation of capital gains and losses in effect before such date.Pub. L. 88–571Oct. 20, 1951designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), limited such subsection to corporations, and added subsec. (b).Pub. L. 88–272

Effective Date of 2010 Amendment, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 111–325, title I, § 101(c)124 Stat. 3538Pub. L. 113–295, div. A, title II, § 205(a)(1)128 Stat. 4025Dec. 22, 2010Dec. 19, 2014“(1).—In generalExcept as provided in paragraphs (2) and (3), the amendments made by this section [amending this section and ] shall apply to net capital losses for taxable years beginning after the date of the enactment of this Act [].section 1222 of this titleDec. 22, 2010“(2).—Coordination rulesSubparagraph (B) of section 1212(a)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as added by this section, shall apply to taxable years beginning after the date of the enactment of this Act.“(3)Excise tax.—“(A).—In generalExcept as provided in subparagraph (B), for purposes of section 4982 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, paragraphs (1) and (2) shall apply by substituting ‘the 1-year periods taken into account under subsection (b)(1)(B) of such section with respect to calendar years beginning after ’ for ‘taxable years beginning after the date of the enactment of this Act’.December 31, 2010“(B).—ElectionA regulated investment company may elect to apply subparagraph (A) by substituting ‘2011’ for ‘2010’. Such election shall be made at such time and in such form and manner as the Secretary of the Treasury (or the Secretary’s delegate) shall prescribe.”

Election Not To Carryback Certain Net Capital Losses, , , as amended by , , , provided that:Pub. L. 91–688, § 384 Stat. 2073Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Jan. 12, 1971Oct. 22, 1986“(a)For purposes of applying section 1212(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] (as amended by section 512 of the Tax Reform Act of 1969) in the case of a corporation which makes an election under subsection (b), any net capital loss sustained in a taxable year beginning after , may not be carried back to any taxable year beginning before , for which it was subject to taxation under section 802 of such Code [], if the carryback of such loss would result in an increase in such corporation’s income tax liability for any such taxable year.December 31, 1969January 1, 1970section 802 of this title“(b)An election to have the provisions of subsection (a) apply shall be made by a corporation—“(1)in such form and manner as the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate may prescribe, and“(2)not later than the time prescribed by law for filing a claim for credit or refund of overpayment of income tax for the first taxable year beginning after , in which such corporation sustains a net capital loss.December 31, 1969“(c)The Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate shall prescribe such regulations as he determines necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.”

children.text: such loss is not attributable to a foreign expropriation capital loss, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the carryback of such loss does not increase or produce a net operating loss (as defined in section 172(c)) for the taxable year to which it is being carried back;

children.label: B
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (C), a capital loss carryover to each of the 5 taxable years succeeding the loss year; and

children.label: C
children.text: a capital loss carryover to each of the 10 taxable years succeeding the loss year, but only to the extent such loss is attributable to a foreign expropriation loss,

children.heading: Foreign expropriation capital loss defined
children.label: A Foreign expropriation capital loss defined
i
children.text: losses sustained directly by reason of the expropriation, intervention, seizure, or similar taking of property by the government of any foreign country, any political subdivision thereof, or any agency or instrumentality of the foregoing, or

children.label: ii
children.text: losses (treated under section 165(g)(1) as losses from the sale or exchange of capital assets) from securities which become worthless by reason of the expropriation, intervention, seizure, or similar taking of property by the government of any foreign country, any political subdivision thereof, or any agency or instrumentality of the foregoing.

children.heading: Portion of loss attributable to foreign expropriation capital loss
children.label: B Portion of loss attributable to foreign expropriation capital loss
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the portion of any net capital loss for any taxable year attributable to a foreign expropriation capital loss is the amount of the foreign expropriation capital loss for such year (but not in excess of the net capital loss for such year).

children.heading: Priority of application
children.label: C Priority of application
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), if a portion of a net capital loss for any taxable year is attributable to a foreign expropriation capital loss, such portion shall be considered to be a separate net capital loss for such year to be applied after the other portion of such net capital loss.

children.label: i
children.text: paragraph (1) shall not apply to such loss,

children.label: ii
children.text: the excess of the net short-term capital loss over the net long-term capital gain for such year shall be a short-term capital loss arising on the first day of the next taxable year, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the excess of the net long-term capital loss over the net short-term capital gain for such year shall be a long-term capital loss arising on the first day of the next taxable year.

children.heading: Losses to which this paragraph applies
children.label: i Losses to which this paragraph applies
children.text: Clauses (ii) and (iii) of subparagraph (A) shall be applied without regard to any amount treated as a short-term capital loss under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Losses to which general rule applies
children.label: ii Losses to which general rule applies
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall be applied by substituting “net capital loss for the loss year or any taxable year thereafter (other than a net capital loss to which paragraph (3)(A) applies)” for “net capital loss for the loss year or any taxable year thereafter”.

children.label: A
children.text: for which it is a regulated investment company (as defined in section 851), or

children.label: B
children.text: for which it is a real estate investment trust (as defined in section 856).

children.label: A
children.text: the excess of the net short-term capital loss over the net long-term capital gain for such year shall be a short-term capital loss in the succeeding taxable year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the excess of the net long-term capital loss over the net short-term capital gain for such year shall be a long-term capital loss in the succeeding taxable year.

children.heading: Treatment of amounts allowed under section 1211(b)(1) or (2)
children.label: 2 Treatment of amounts allowed under section 1211(b)(1) or (2)
i
children.text: the amount allowed for the taxable year under paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1211(b), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the adjusted taxable income for such taxable year.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount allowed for the taxable year under paragraph (1) or (2) of section 1211(b), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the deduction allowed for such year under section 151 or any deduction in lieu thereof.

children.heading: Carryback of losses from section 1256 contracts to offset prior gains from such contracts
children.label: c Carryback of losses from section 1256 contracts to offset prior gains from such contracts
A
children.text: shall be a carryback to each of the 3 taxable years preceding the loss year, and

children.label: i
children.text: 40 percent of the amount so allowed shall be treated as a short-term capital loss from section 1256 contracts, and

children.label: ii
children.text: 60 percent of the amount so allowed shall be treated as a long-term capital loss from section 1256 contracts.

children.heading: Amount carried to each taxable year
children.label: 2 Amount carried to each taxable year
children.text: The entire amount of the net section 1256 contracts loss for any taxable year shall be carried to the earliest of the taxable years to which such loss may be carried back under paragraph (1). The portion of such loss which shall be carried to each of the 2 other taxable years to which such loss may be carried back shall be the excess (if any) of such loss over the portion of such loss which, after the application of paragraph (3), was allowed as a carryback for any prior taxable year.

children.heading: Amount which may be used in any prior taxable year
children.label: 3 Amount which may be used in any prior taxable year
A
children.text: such amount does not exceed the net section 1256 contract gain for such year, and

children.label: B
children.text: the allowance of such carryback does not increase or produce a net operating loss (as defined in section 172(c)) for such year.

children.label: A
children.text: the net capital loss for the taxable year determined by taking into account only gains and losses from section 1256 contracts, or

children.label: B
children.text: the sum of the amounts which, but for paragraph (6)(A), would be treated as capital losses in the succeeding taxable year under subparagraphs (A) and (B) of subsection (b)(1).

children.label: i
children.text: the capital gain net income for the taxable year determined by taking into account only gains and losses from section 1256 contracts, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the capital gain net income for the taxable year.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: The net section 1256 contract gain for any taxable year before the loss year shall be computed without regard to the net section 1256 contracts loss for the loss year or for any taxable year thereafter.

children.heading: Coordination with carryforward provisions of subsection (b)(1)
children.label: 6 Coordination with carryforward provisions of subsection (b)(1)

children.heading: Carryforward amount reduced by amount used as carryback
children.label: A Carryforward amount reduced by amount used as carryback
i
children.text: 40 percent of the amount allowed as a carryback shall be treated as a short-term capital gain for the loss year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: 60 percent of the amount allowed as a carryback shall be treated as a long-term capital gain for the loss year.

children.heading: Carryover loss retains character as attributable to section 1256 contract
children.label: B Carryover loss retains character as attributable to section 1256 contract
children.text: Any amount carried forward as a short-term or long-term capital loss to any taxable year under subsection (b)(1) (after the application of subparagraph (A)) shall, to the extent attributable to losses from section 1256 contracts, be treated as loss from section 1256 contracts for such taxable year.

children.heading: Section 1256 contract
children.label: A Section 1256 contract
children.text: The term “section 1256 contract” means any section 1256 contract (as defined in section 1256(b)) to which section 1256 applies.

children.heading: Exclusion for estates and trusts
children.label: B Exclusion for estates and trusts
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to any estate or trust.
